title: Underground Live Map
author: @dracos
description: Live map of London Underground trains and buses using the TfL API to plot approximate real-time locations of all trains on the network. Hosted at traintimes.org.uk/map/tube/.

title: London Pathfinder
author: @Vor7reX
description: Tactical radar-style dashboard for the TfL network with a C++17 pathfinding engine. Visualises Dijkstra vs A* algorithm traversal node-by-node with glassmorphism UI and dynamic line-color coding.

title: TfL JamCams Live Traffic Map
author: @OldManBrook
description: Google Maps PWA showing 900+ TfL traffic CCTV cameras, live road incidents, and real-time bus, Underground and National Rail departures. Ad-free and available at tfljamcams.net.

title: London Tube Timetable Visualiser
author: @ruaridhw
description: Data science project visualising TfL network timetable data with spatiotemporal animation of train movement and journey planner algorithms. Compares the iconic schematic tube map to geographic reality.

title: d3-tube-map
author: @johnwalley
description: D3.js library for drawing tube-style schematic maps in the style of the London Underground. Used by many community projects for creative custom network visualisations.

title: TfL Santander Bike Journey Analysis
author: @charlie1347
description: Visualisation of 13+ million predicted London Santander Cycle ('Boris Bikes') routes using datashader, including analysis of how tube strikes spike cycling demand across the city.

title: Animated London Tube Pathfinding
author: @ZeevoX
description: Uses the official TfL tube SVG map to visually animate pathfinding algorithms (BFS, DFS etc.) station-by-station, making algorithm education interactive and city-specific.

title: London GeoJSON Map Visualisation (D3)
author: @Anas1108
description: Interactive London GeoJSON data explorer built with D3, featuring map, graph, and timeline views with advanced pan, zoom, select, brush and linked-view interactions.

title: London Greenground Cycle Map (2025 Edition)
author: Helen Ilus
description: Tube-map-styled schematic connecting London's National Cycle Network, Cycleways and green spaces. The 2025 edition is the first city-wide map linking cycle routes to over 1,000 parks and waterways.

title: TfL Station Entry & Exit Data Visualisation
author: @Osakwe1
description: Jupyter Notebook project exploring and visualising TfL station entry/exit patterns from 2007--2021, producing interactive charts, maps and network evolution visuals.

title: TfL Quix JamCam Computer Vision Demo
author: @quixio
description: Real-time ML pipeline that captures live TfL JamCam CCTV feeds and uses YOLOv8 to detect and count vehicles at each camera location, displayed on a live map.

title: Mapped-based London Data Visualisations (vis)
author: @oobrien
description: Collection of map-based data visualisations of London and transit/transport data by Oliver O'Brien, including the well-known Tube Creature and other TfL open data projects.

title: Oyster Card Journey Analysis & Visualisation
author: @ZaraTam
description: Origin and destination analysis of TfL Oyster card journey data across London boroughs using Python, with interactive maps and visualisations of tube travel patterns.

title: Dijkstra London Tube Interactive Visualiser
author: @tusharpandey13
description: ReactJS web app that interactively visualises Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm on the London Underground network, with edge weights based on real average train speeds.

title: TrackMyTube -- TfL Live Status Platform
author: TrackMyTube
description: Web platform for real-time TfL train times, live service status, and travel news across the London Underground, bus, DLR, and Overground networks.
